name: Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic (1940–1953)
description: A plain red field with the name of the republic inscribed in gold across the upper hoist in Cyrillic — in Tajik (РСС Тоҷикистон) and Russian (Таджикская ССР).
history: Adopted after the Tajik language switched to the Cyrillic alphabet on 28 September 1940, replacing the earlier Latin-script inscription. It remained in use until the modern Soviet triband flag was introduced on 20 March 1953.

features.symbolism: the name of the Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic
